edibility: Inedible — woody, fibrous, hard texture with no culinary value, and not toxic but completely unsuitable for consumption.
taste: Not consumed due to the hard, woody nature of the fruiting body.
search: Phellinus ferruginosus appears year-round on the undersides of fallen hardwood branches and logs. Identify by the distinctive rusty-brown to rust-red pore surface and tough, bracket-like form. Distinguish from similar polypores by the characteristic rusty coloration and perennial fruiting bodies.
